user.title: Set up Ninja Woodfire
user.description: Select a nearby Ninja Woodfire device or enter a Bluetooth address manually.

bluetooth_confirm.title: Ninja Woodfire found
bluetooth_confirm.description:
Do you want to add **{name}** ({address}) to Home Assistant?

Only confirm if this is your device.

abort.not_ninja_device: This device does not appear to be a Ninja Woodfire.

init.title: Ninja Woodfire options
init.description:
Device: {address}

You can rename this device. To remove it, use the device page in Home Assistant.

passive_scanning.title: Active Bluetooth scanning required for {name}
passive_scanning.description:
The Ninja Woodfire grill sends half of its status in a BLE scan response, which is only transmitted when the Bluetooth adapter uses active scanning. Home Assistant is currently only receiving partial advertisements, so the grill's state cannot be decoded.

To fix this: go to **Settings → Devices & Services → Bluetooth**, click **Configure** on your Bluetooth adapter, and set **Scanning mode** to **Active**. If the grill is received through an ESPHome Bluetooth proxy, enable active scanning in that proxy's configuration instead.
